name: Springerle
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 40 minutes Standing time: 1 hour plus overnight
Cooking time: 10 to 12 minutes Aging time: 1 week or more
1. If Baker's ammonia is not powdered, mash it with a rolling pin.
Dissolve in milk in a small bowl and let stand for 1 hour before using.
2. Beat eggs in large bowl of electric mixer until thick and lemon-colored (about 5 minutes).
Gradually beat in confectioners' sugar until creamy and smooth.
Add butter and beat again until creamy.
Add anise oil, dissolved Baker's ammonia, and salt; beat to mix.
Gradually beat in enough flour to make a stiff dough.
3. Work pieces of dough on a floured work surface until stiff enough to roll out
and hold the design of the springerle rolling pin or mold.
Roll out on lightly floured board with floured rolling pin to 1/4-inch thickness.
Press design on dough with floured springerle rolling pin or mold.
Cut cookies apart using floured knife. Cover work surface with clean kitchen towel overnight.
4. The next day, preheat oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
Bake cookies on greased baking sheets until barely golden on the bottom (10 to 12 minutes).
Cool on wire racks. Store in tightly covered tins and allow to mellow at least 1 week before serving.
Note: This recipe can also be made using 1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
(replacing milk and Baker's ammonia) and anise extract instead of anise oil.
However, cookies will not have the same delicate texture and anise flavor will be less rich.
If using baking powder, add it with salt to the batter.
